mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A statistically significant negative correlation (p < 0.05) is observed between RSAFS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, evidenced by the unemployment rate falling below a key psychological threshold, signals strong economic health and increasing consumer purchasing power, which typically precedes positive equity market performance over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE falls below 5.0%, the average S&P 500 60-day forward return is not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) compared to other periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low inflation and an accommodative federal funds rate creates an ideal environment for equity markets by reducing discount rates and supporting corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat, with a mean return not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial expansion, positive momentum in industrial production signals robust manufacturing activity and corporate earnings, leading to immediate positive market reactions and S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified industrial expansion phases (e.g., INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3%), the S&P 500 5-day forward returns following an INDPRO release are not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of housing starts above a critical threshold indicates strong consumer and business confidence in the economic outlook, driving construction-related activity and broader economic growth, which supports equity markets over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST consistently remains above 1200.0 (3-month average), the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Continuous job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, signifies a healthy and expanding economy with strong consumer purchasing power, which provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate earnings and short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of very low federal funds rates and solid GDP growth indicates an environment of ample liquidity and real economic expansion, which is highly supportive of equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative policy
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, robust retail sales growth signifies genuine demand strength that translates into real corporate revenue growth, rather than just price increases, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2.0% and RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.10).
